learning_outcomes.description: Analyse Human-Computer Interaction from historical and theoretical perspectives
Relate cognitive, social, behavioural, and emotional theories to the design of effective human-system interaction.
Analyse human-centred design issues by applying a range of design research methods, which involve the people from target user groups, in the process of interactive system design.
Apply HCI techniques and principles of user-interface design in the design of an interactive system.
Select and apply appropriate HCI evaluation (user and expert) techniques to evaluate interactive systems and analyse the results of those evaluation methods.
Apply HCI knowledge, in terms of system accessibility, within appropriate user-interface design for a broad range of user groups.
Develop collaborative design outcomes through both shared team and individual work.

Demonstrate an understanding of relevant HCI research by identifying, inspecting, and critiquing appropriate literature and with appropriate referencing [THIS LEARNING OBJECTIVE CONTRIBUTES TO AQF9 RESEARCH FOR MASTERS PROGRAMS]

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
In teams, you will work through a human-centred design process to propose a design for an interactive system (e.g., an app, web app, website, AR/VR app, or other system). A specific, detailed task sheet will be provided in class. The focus of this work is on the process that your team goes through to get to your final design.

There will be specific touch points in studio sessions for you to get formative feedback en route to the final submission (in week 3 for topic selection and in week 5 for initial assumptions).

Final submission of the whole project will be in Week 9.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: For the final design proposal, you should submit a single electronic file via Blackboard on or before the due date/time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Each student will appraise and assess an existing interface using a range of HCI theories and techniques. Students will (1) perform an expert evaluation/review, applying established HCI principles (e.g., Nielsen’s heuristics) to diagnose usability; and, (2) run a user-based evaluation, gathering direct feedback from users. Findings will be analysed and linked back to HCI theory, culminating in recommendations for future improvements. This approach develops essential evaluation skills and ensures accessibility considerations remain at the forefront.

Students will produce a report that includes evidence of the inquiry, including a justification of the methods chosen and recommendations for what they would change and why.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You should submit a single electronic file on Blackboard on or before the due date/time. Please ensure that you include a completed and signed coversheet to the beginning of your work before it is submitted. Your submission must be a single document that contains your assignment, including a signed coversheet. Your submission should be a pdf.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Students will be asked to produce a social media post (for Instagram or LinkedIn) that translates a recent piece of UX research into actionable advice for UX practitioners.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You should submit a single electronic file on Blackboard on or before the due date/time. Please ensure that you include a completed and signed coversheet to the beginning of your work before it is submitted. Your submission must be a single document that contains your assignment, including a signed coversheet. Your submission should be a pdf.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final end of semester exam will test understanding of the concepts covered over the entire course. The exam will be an invigilated on-campus exam that meets the identity verified assessment requirement. The format will be Multiple-choice and Short answer.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Weekly Studio Sessions

Studios will be delivered in-person (face-to-face). Students will work with demonstrators and staff on their team projects and related activities that support the assessment deliverables and core concepts from the course.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Weekly Studio Sessions Studios will be delivered in-person (face-to-face). Students will work with demonstrators and staff on their team projects and related activities that support the assessment deliverables and core concepts from the course.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture

Lectures will be delivered in-person (face-to-face) and will cover relevant topics in Human-Computer Interaction. Topics may include, but are not limited to, the interaction design process, interaction fundamentals, ethics, mental models, conceptual design, cognition, interaction paradigms, evaluating usability, etc.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06, L07, L08
